engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz CLK Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz CLK across all C208/A208 and C209/A209 generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes-Benz 3.0-litre V6 turbodiesel introduced in 2005. Renowned for its smooth power delivery but plagued by oil cooler seal failures that require extensive labour to repair.
compatibleVariants: CLK320 CDI Sport, CLK320 CDI Elegance, CLK320 CDI Avantgarde
commonFailures: Oil cooler seal leakage - requires removal of turbocharger and intake manifold
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The 2.1-litre four-cylinder turbodiesel that powered entry-level CLK diesel variants. A robust engine when maintained, but injector seals are a known weak point in higher-mileage units.
compatibleVariants: CLK220 CDI, CLK220 CDI Sport, CLK220 CDI Elegance
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM646 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: The five-cylinder turbodiesel that delivered exceptional torque and reliability in early C209 CLKs. A cult favourite among diesel enthusiasts despite its age.
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM612 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: The 3.5-litre V6 introduced in the CLK350 facelift. Delivers 272hp but is infamous for the balance shaft sprocket failure that defined an era of Mercedes V6 ownership .
compatibleVariants: CLK350, CLK350 Sport, CLK350 Elegance, CLK350 Avantgarde
commonFailures: Balance shaft idler gear wear - fault codes P0016/P0017
entries.cta: Get quotes for M272 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The 3.0-litre version of the M272 V8 family, powering the CLK280. Shares the balance shaft vulnerability of its larger sibling but remains a popular replacement choice.
commonFailures: Balance shaft sprocket failure (engine serial numbers below #468933)
entries.cta: Get quotes for M272 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: The bulletproof 3.2-litre V6 that powered pre-facelift CLK320 models. One of Mercedes’ most reliable V6 engines, still sought after by buyers avoiding M272 balance shaft issues.
compatibleVariants: CLK320 (C208), CLK320 (C209), CLK320 Elegance, CLK320 Avantgarde
entries.cta: Get quotes for M112 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.6L Petrol
entries.history: The 2.6-litre entry-level V6 for C209 CLK240 buyers wanting six-cylinder smoothness without the running costs of the 3.2-litre unit.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M112 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The 1.8-litre supercharged four-cylinder that made CLK200 Kompressor ownership accessible. Timing chain and camshaft adjuster issues are the main replacement drivers in high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: CLK200 Kompressor, CLK200 Kompressor Sport, CLK200 Kompressor Elegance
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and sprocket wear - often requires full replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for M271 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 2.3L Petrol
entries.history: The supercharged 2.3-litre four-cylinder that powered the early CLK230 Kompressor. A robust engine with fewer electronic complications than later M271 units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M111 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 5.4L Petrol
entries.history: The 5.4-litre V8 that powered the CLK55 AMG - a naturally aspirated legend known for brutal torque and surprising reliability.
commonFailures: Piston ring wear in high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L Petrol
entries.history: The 5.0-litre V8 that brought AMG-rivalling performance to standard CLK500 models without the maintenance premium of full AMG ownership.
commonFailures: Airmatic compressor failure (not engine-related but common on V8 models)
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 4.3L Petrol
entries.history: The 4.3-litre V8 that powered the CLK430 in C208 generation, offering V8 prestige at a more accessible price point than the full-fat AMG.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

entries.title: 6.2L Petrol
entries.history: Mercedes-AMG’s first fully in-house developed V8. 6.2 litres of naturally aspirated fury producing 481hp. A modern classic that demands specialist attention.
compatibleVariants: CLK63 AMG, CLK63 AMG Black Series
commonFailures: Head bolt failure - early engines prone to bolts breaking, allowing coolant into combustion chambers
entries.cta: Get quotes for M156 Mercedes Benz CLK engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz CLK Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes CLK350 / CLK280 M272 Balance Shaft Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes CLK350 / CLK280 M272 Balance Shaft Failure - The Single Biggest V6 Problem
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes CLK280, CLK350 (C209 2005-2009, M272 E30 & M272 E35 engines)
problems.rootCause: The balance shaft idler gear sprocket was manufactured from a soft metal that wears prematurely. As the sprocket teeth wear down, the timing chain becomes slack, causing incorrect camshaft timing and triggering fault codes P0016/P0017/P0018/P0019 .

repairOptions.tier: Full Timing Chain & Balance Shaft Replacement - Complete repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of balance shaft sprocket, timing chain, tensioners, guides. Engine does not need full removal but requires extensive front-end disassembly
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-100k miles if upgraded hardened sprockets fitted. Only viable if caught before catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete replacement engine with upgraded balance shaft components, bench tested, supply & fit, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles with upgraded components. Best option when engine has already suffered timing-related valve damage

problems.recommendation: On a CLK280 or CLK350 worth £3,000-£6,000, the repair cost can approach the vehicle's value. If the car is in excellent condition and you catch the problem early (P0016/P0017 codes with no drivability issues), specialist repair at £2,000-£2,500 is worthwhile. However, if the timing chain has slipped and caused valve damage - or if the car has other major issues - we recommend replacement with a rebuilt unit that has the upgraded sprockets factory-fitted.
problems.cta: Compare M272 rebuilt engine prices for your Mercedes CLK

problems.group: Mercedes CLK200 Kompressor M271 Timing Chain & Camshaft Adjuster Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes CLK200 Kompressor M271 Timing Chain & Camshaft Adjuster Failure
problems.rootCause: The M271 engine uses a timing chain and camshaft sprockets with plastic guide rails that become brittle. The camshaft adjuster solenoid magnets fail, and the chain stretches, causing startup rattle and potential jumping of timing .

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Camshaft Adjuster Magnet & Solenoid Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of failed camshaft adjuster magnets and solenoids. Addresses fault code P0020 (camshaft position actuator circuit)
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-40k miles. Only works if timing chain itself hasn't stretched

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of timing chain, chain tensioner, camshaft sprockets, and guide rails
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles with upgraded components. Requires specialist timing tools

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete replacement engine with revised timing components, bench tested, supply & fit, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles with upgraded timing gear. Best when chain has already jumped

problems.recommendation: The M271 engine is known for making a "death rattle" on cold starts. If you only hear a brief rattle (1-2 seconds), the chain tensioner may be the culprit - a £200-£300 specialist fix. But if the rattle lasts longer or you have fault codes for camshaft correlation, immediate attention is required. Replacement with a rebuilt unit that has factory-upgraded timing components is often the most cost-effective long-term solution for high-mileage CLK200Ks.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M271 Mercedes CLK200 Kompressor engine replacement

problems.group: Mercedes CLK 7G-TRONIC Transmission Conductor Plate Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes CLK 7G-TRONIC Transmission Conductor Plate Failure - Often Mistaken for Engine Trouble
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes CLK280, CLK320 CDI, CLK350, CLK500, CLK55 AMG (2005-2009, 7G-TRONIC 722.9 gearbox)
problems.rootCause: The conductor plate (electro-hydraulic control unit) inside the 7G-TRONIC transmission fails due to faulty speed sensors. Symptoms include gearbox stuck in gear, harsh shifting, transmission warning light, and the car entering "limp mode" - which many owners initially mistake for engine failure .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of conductor plate (TCM) with revised unit, transmission fluid, filter, and sump gasket. Specialist removal and coding required
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-100k miles with OEM replacement part. Most common and cost-effective solution

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete reconditioned 7G-TRONIC gearbox with new conductor plate, torque converter, and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles with fully rebuilt unit. Best when internal mechanical damage has occurred

problems.recommendation: Don't replace the engine if your CLK is showing transmission symptoms. The 7G-TRONIC conductor plate failure is extremely common and produces check engine lights and poor drivability that mimic engine problems. A specialist can diagnose with STAR diagnostic equipment. The conductor plate replacement at £550-£850 is almost always the fix - far cheaper than any engine work. The valve body can also be repaired rather than replaced for around £430 + VAT .
problems.cta: Compare Mercedes CLK transmission repair vs replacement costs

problems.group: Mercedes CLK320 CDI OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes CLK320 CDI OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure - The Diesel V6 Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes CLK320 CDI (C209 2005-2009, OM642 3.0 V6 diesel)
problems.rootCause: The rubber oil cooler seals at the centre of the Vee engine degrade and leak. Oil mixes with coolant or leaks externally, causing low oil pressure warnings and potential engine damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Oil Cooler Seal Replacement - Preventative repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of oil cooler seals, intake manifold removal, cleaning of EGR system and inlet ports. Requires special tools and 8-10 hours labour
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles with genuine Mercedes upgraded seals. Significant labour cost but saves engine

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete replacement engine with new oil cooler seals, turbo, injectors tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles with all known OM642 weaknesses addressed in rebuild

problems.recommendation: OM642 oil cooler seal failure is one of the most common diesel V6 problems across the entire Mercedes range - CLK320 CDI, E320 CDI, ML320 CDI all suffer the same. The repair requires removing the entire intake manifold, which is labour-intensive but not catastrophic. On a well-maintained CLK320 CDI, paying £700-£1,200 for specialist seal replacement is excellent value. However, if the engine has been run low on oil or the coolant is contaminated, internal damage may have occurred - in which case a rebuilt unit is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare OM642 CLK320 CDI rebuilt engine prices

problems.h4: Mercedes CLK Kompressor Supercharger Failure - C208 & Early C209
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes CLK200 Kompressor (C208 1997-2002, M111), CLK230 Kompressor (C208 1997-2002, M111)
problems.rootCause: The supercharger (kompressor) units develop bearing and seal wear. Symptoms include whistling or squealing noises on acceleration, loss of power, increased oil consumption, and fault codes P0299 (boost pressure too low) .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Specialist rebuild of kompressor unit with new bearings, seals, and clutch service
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-80k miles with quality rebuild. Far cheaper than replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of complete supercharger assembly with reconditioned unit
Complete replacement engine with serviced supercharger, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best when engine has other issues or kompressor failure has caused internal damage

problems.recommendation: The M111 kompressor engine is otherwise robust. On a C208 CLK200 or CLK230 with a failed supercharger but otherwise healthy engine, specialist rebuild for £500-£900 is the right answer - far more cost-effective than replacing the whole engine. However, if the supercharger has failed catastrophically and sent debris into the engine, a replacement engine may be required. For C209 facelift models with the M271 engine, see the timing chain section above.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CLK Kompressor engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes CLK
finalCta.paragraph: The CLK is now 15-23 years old - its value sits firmly in mainstream affordability territory, but that doesn't mean a failed engine should send yours to the scrapyard. Whether you're facing M272 balance shaft failure, M271 timing chain rattle, OM642 oil leaks, or a failed kompressor, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes CLK specialists. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point, bench testing before delivery, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes CLK Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes CLK and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz CLK Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on your CLK variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Factory-spec engine rebuilt to original tolerances with new piston rings, bearings and seals. Oem-grade quality.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec engine rebuilt to original tolerances with new piston rings, bearings and seals. Oem-grade quality.
types.backDescription: A factory reconditioned CLK engine returns your vehicle to original Mercedes-Benz specification.
backBullets: M272 and OM642 units remanufactured with upgraded balance shaft sprockets and oil cooler seals
Fully disassembled, ultrasonically cleaned and measured to factory tolerances
Ideal for CLK350 and CLK320 CDI owners wanting dealer-level quality without dealer pricing
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Mercedes Benz CLK engine prices

types.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Professionally rebuilt engine with new gaskets, seals, timing components and wear parts fitted to serviceable block.
types.frontDescription: Professionally rebuilt engine with new gaskets, seals, timing components and wear parts fitted to serviceable block.
types.backDescription: A rebuilt CLK engine addresses known failure points while keeping your original block and cylinder head.
backBullets: Timing chain and tensioner replacement standard on M271 and M272 rebuilds
Cylinder heads pressure tested and valve seals renewed on all V6 and Kompressor units
Most cost-effective solution for CLK200K and CLK240 with high-mileage wear
types.cta: Get rebuilt Mercedes Benz CLK engine quotes

types.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage salvaged engine removed from a donor CLK, compression tested and visually inspected before supply.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage salvaged engine removed from a donor CLK, compression tested and visually inspected before supply.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and history vary by donor.
types.backDescription: A used CLK engine offers the fastest turnaround but carries higher risk of unknown wear.
backBullets: Best suited to pre-facelift C208 models with M112 or M111 engines where parts remain plentiful
Choose engines from rear-end donor vehicles with documented mileage where possible
Not recommended for M272 balance shaft affected engines unless verified post-recall production
types.cta: Check used Mercedes Benz CLK engine availability

types.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Like-new engine rebuilt to beyond factory tolerances with upgraded components addressing original design flaws.
types.frontDescription: Like-new engine rebuilt to beyond factory tolerances with upgraded components addressing original design flaws.
types.backDescription: A remanufactured CLK engine is the gold standard - better than new where original designs had weaknesses.
backBullets: M272 reman units include hardened balance shaft sprockets that eliminate the P0016 failure permanently
OM642 reman includes revised oil cooler seals and upgraded turbo feed lines
Each engine hot-tested before delivery with full 12-month unlimited mileage cover
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Mercedes Benz CLK engine costs

types.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Refurbished Engines
types.description: Cleaned, inspected engine with failed components replaced but not fully stripped to bare block. Mid-range option.
types.frontDescription: Cleaned, inspected engine with failed components replaced but not fully stripped to bare block. Mid-range option.
types.backDescription: A refurbished CLK engine sits between used and rebuilt - addressing known failures without full disassembly.
backBullets: Focused on top-end refresh - cylinder head gasket, timing gear, valve seals
Suitable for CLK230 Kompressor (M111) where bottom ends are generally robust
Faster turnaround than full rebuild but verify exactly which components were replaced

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz CLK Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the CLK across C208/A208 (1997-2002) and C209/A209 (2002-2010) generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, variants, years and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1-litre four-cylinder turbodiesel (OM646) arrived in 2005 as the entry-level diesel for the facelift C209 CLK220 CDI. It offered economical running costs with 148bhp and 340Nm of torque, making it a popular choice for daily drivers seeking lower fuel bills without sacrificing Mercedes refinement .
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve carbon build-up at higher mileages
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLK 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.7-litre five-cylinder turbodiesel (OM612) powered the CLK270 CDI between 2002 and 2005. This engine is something of a cult favourite among diesel enthusiasts, delivering 170bhp and 400Nm of torque with a distinctive five-cylinder soundtrack that sets it apart from conventional four-cylinder diesels .
commonFailurePoints: injector seal leakage on higher-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLK 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 turbodiesel (OM642) arrived with the CLK320 CDI in 2005. Producing 221bhp and 415Nm of torque, it transformed the CLK into a genuine grand tourer with effortless mid-range punch and surprisingly refined manners for a diesel V6 .
commonFailurePoints: Oil cooler seal leakage requiring intake manifold removal
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLK 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre supercharged four-cylinder (M271) arrived with the C209 CLK200 Kompressor from 2002 onwards, producing an eager 163bhp from just 1.8 litres thanks to its Lysholm-type supercharger. This engine made the CLK accessible to a wider audience, offering coupé style with four-cylinder running costs .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 2.0-litre four-cylinder (M111) powered the earliest C208 CLK200 models between 1997 and 2002. With 136bhp, it was the entry-level petrol option - underpowered by modern standards but robust and straightforward to maintain .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 2.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.3-litre supercharged four-cylinder (M111) powered the C208 CLK230 Kompressor from 1997 to 2002. Delivering 193bhp and 280Nm of torque, it offered serious performance for its era - catapulting the CLK from 0-60mph in just 8.4 seconds. The distinctive supercharger whine is still loved by enthusiasts today .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 2.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 2.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.6-litre V6 (M112) powered the CLK240 across both C208 and early C209 generations. Introduced in 2002 as the entry-level V6 option, it produced 170bhp and provided the smooth six-cylinder refinement that buyers expected from a Mercedes-Benz coupé .
commonFailurePoints: balance shaft wear on early production units
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 2.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre V6 (M272) arrived with the CLK280 in 2005 as part of the C209 facelift. Producing 228bhp, it replaced the older 2.6-litre unit and brought modern electronic management and improved fuel efficiency - though it also introduced the infamous balance shaft vulnerability .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.2-litre V6 (M112) was the flagship six-cylinder for the C208 and early C209 CLK320. Delivering 218bhp, this engine was renowned for its silky-smooth power delivery and outstanding reliability - making it a favourite among owners who valued substance over outright pace .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 3.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.5-litre V6 (M272) powered the CLK350 from 2005 onwards, producing a healthy 272bhp and 350Nm of torque. As the most powerful non-AMG V6 available, it could sprint from 0-60mph in just 6.7 seconds - but its reputation was permanently damaged by the balance shaft failures that afflicted early production examples .
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft idler gear wear (fault codes P0016/P0017)
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 4.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.3-litre V8 (M113) powered the C208 CLK430 between 1998 and 2002. With 279bhp and 400Nm of torque, it offered genuine performance without the full AMG price premium. The M113 V8 is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most robust modern engines .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 4.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.0-litre V8 (M113) powered the C209 CLK500 from 2002 onwards, producing 306bhp and 460Nm of torque. This engine brought near-AMG performance to the standard CLK range, propelling the car from 0-60mph in just 5.9 seconds .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 5.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 5.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.4-litre V8 (M113) powered both C208 and C209 CLK55 AMG models. Producing 347-360bhp depending on year, this naturally aspirated V8 was the heart of Mercedes-AMG's first proper CLK. Its blend of effortless torque and surprising reliability has made it a modern classic .
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring wear in high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 5.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.2-litre V8 (M156) powered the CLK63 AMG from 2006 to 2009, producing 481bhp - or 500bhp in the limited-run Black Series. As AMG's first entirely in-house designed V8, the M156 is a masterpiece of modern engine engineering, albeit one with known head bolt vulnerabilities .
commonFailurePoints: Head bolt failure (coolant ingress to cylinders)
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLK 6.2L petrol engine prices

All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes CLK specialists.

fuelTypes.tag: Mercedes Benz CLK Engines Fuel Type
fuelTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz CLK Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Mercedes Benz CLK engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the early C208 diesel to the final C209 AMG V8.

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power arrived with the C209 generation in 2002 and ran through to 2010, with the OM642 V6 in the CLK320 CDI becoming the most sought-after oil-burner for its effortless torque and motorway refinement.
items.descriptor: Diesel power arrived with the C209 generation in 2002 and ran through to 2010, with the OM642 V6 in the CLK320 CDI becoming the most sought-after oil-burner for its effortless torque and motorway refinement.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz CLK Diesel Engine Families
families: OM642 - 3.0 V6 turbodiesel, CLK320 CDI, known for oil cooler seal failures
OM646 - 2.1 four-cylinder, CLK220 CDI, reliable but injector seals leak
OM612 - 2.7 five-cylinder, CLK270 CDI, cult classic with turbo bearing issues
knownFor: Exceptional motorway fuel economy - 45-50mpg realistic
OM642 oil cooler seal failure requires intake manifold removal
High replacement demand on CLK320 CDI due to V6 refinement
Lower running costs than petrol equivalents
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLK Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Petrol Engines
items.description: The CLK’s petrol range spans four-cylinder Kompressor units, smooth V6s from the M112 and M272 families, and thunderous V8s in CLK500 and AMG guises - covering everything from economy to 500bhp hero cars.
items.descriptor: The CLK’s petrol range spans four-cylinder Kompressor units, smooth V6s from the M112 and M272 families, and thunderous V8s in CLK500 and AMG guises - covering everything from economy to 500bhp hero cars.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz CLK Petrol Engine Families
families: M272 - 3.0 and 3.5 V6, CLK280/CLK350, infamous for balance shaft failure
M112 - 2.6 and 3.2 V6, CLK240/CLK320, bulletproof pre-facelift units
M271 - 1.8 Kompressor, CLK200K, timing chain and camshaft adjuster issues
M111 - 2.0 and 2.3 Kompressor, C208 CLK200/CLK230, supercharger clutch wear
M113 - 4.3, 5.0 and 5.4 V8, CLK430/CLK500/CLK55 AMG, robust but valve seals wear
M156 - 6.2 V8, CLK63 AMG, head bolt failure risk on early cars
knownFor: M272 balance shaft sprocket wear (P0016/P0017) on 2005-2007 engines
M271 timing chain rattle on startup - common on CLK200K
M112 and M113 generally regarded as Mercedes' most reliable modern engines
CLK63 AMG M156 is a modern classic with head bolt concerns
Wide price spread from £950 (used 1.8) to £9,000 (rebuilt 6.2)

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLK Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz CLK Hybrid / Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: No 48-volt MHEV systems on any CLK generation
No plug-in hybrid (PHEV) CLK ever produced
Replacement engines are standard internal combustion units only

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz CLK Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mercedes CLK’s production timeline from 1997 to 2010, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: C208 Launch: Four & Six-Cylinder Petrol Range
years.description: The CLK debuted in June 1997, sharing the W202 C-Class platform but wearing W210 E-Class styling - and launching a new market niche for Mercedes-Benz .
keyChanges: CLK launched in coupé form (C208) as E-Class coupé replacement
Three initial engines: 2.0-litre naturally aspirated four-cylinder, 2.0-litre supercharged, 2.3-litre supercharged
CLK 320 V6 (3.2-litre, 218bhp) joined within months of launch
Elegance and Sport trim levels offered
knownFor: Elegant pillar-less coupé styling with distinctive E-Class face
M111 supercharged engines offer strong torque but supercharger clutches fail
M112 V6 is one of Mercedes’ most reliable engines of the era
Convertible (A208) launched June 1998
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-1999 Mercedes CLK replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes CLK 200 engine replacement • Mercedes CLK 320 engine price • CLK 230 Kompressor rebuilt engine

years.preview: C208 Facelift: V8 Power & AMG Arrive
years.description: The 1999 facelift brought revised styling, Tiptronic automatic gearboxes, and two game-changing V8s - the CLK 430 and the first-ever CLK 55 AMG .
keyChanges: Facelift introduced new bumpers, side skirts, wing mirror indicators
CLK 430 V8 (4.3-litre, 279bhp) launched in 1999
CLK 55 AMG (5.4-litre V8, 347bhp) debuted as Europe-only model in 2000
2000 engine refresh: 200 Kompressor detuned from 192hp to 163hp (EVO engine)
Six-speed manual transmission introduced on Kompressor models
knownFor: CLK 55 AMG remains a modern classic with 5.4-litre naturally aspirated V8
M113 V8s are robust - valve seal wear is main age-related issue
2000-onwards Kompressor engines more reliable but 30hp down on earlier cars
Convertible continued until March 2003, overlapping with C209
years.cta: Compare prices for 1999-2002 Mercedes CLK engines
years.ticker: CLK 430 engine replacement cost • Mercedes CLK 55 AMG engine price • M113 rebuilt V8 price • CLK 200 Kompressor timing chain

years.preview: C209 Launch: New Platform, Wider Diesel Choice
years.description: The second-generation CLK arrived in Autumn 2002 on a stiffer W203 C-Class platform - bringing pillar-less side windows, vastly improved handling, and the first diesel CLKs .
keyChanges: All-new C209 coupé (2002), convertible (A209) followed in 2003
Stiffer platform based on post-2000 C-Class
First diesel CLK: CLK 270 CDI (2.7-litre five-cylinder, OM612)
Petrol range: 1.8 supercharged M271, 2.6 V6 M112, 3.2 V6 M112, 5.0 V8 M113
CLK 55 AMG continued with uprated 5.4 V8 (367hp)
Avantgarde replaced Sport trim level
mainEngines: OM612 DE27 LA - 2.7 five-cylinder diesel (CLK 270 CDI, 170hp)
knownFor: CLK 270 CDI five-cylinder diesel - cult favourite with 400Nm torque
M112 2.6 V6 (CLK 240) is “rather unloved” - underpowered for a V6
M271 timing chain issues begin appearing on higher-mileage examples
Diesels available on coupé only - cabriolet remained petrol-only
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2005 Mercedes CLK replacement engines
years.ticker: CLK 270 CDI engine replacement • M271 timing chain replacement CLK • CLK 200 Kompressor engine price • CLK 500 V8 rebuilt cost

years.preview: C209 Facelift: M272 V6 & Common-Rail Diesels
years.description: The 2005 facelift brought “Direct Control” technology, sharper steering, and a completely overhauled engine line-up - including the infamous M272 V6 and the superb OM642 V6 diesel .
keyChanges: CLK 280 (3.0 M272, 231hp) replaced the unloved CLK 240
CLK 350 (3.5 M272, 272hp) slotted between CLK 280 and CLK 500
CLK 320 CDI (3.0 OM642 V6 diesel, 224hp) arrived - first V6 diesel in CLK
CLK 220 CDI (2.1 OM646 four-cylinder diesel, 150hp) offered lower running costs
7G-TRONIC seven-speed automatic introduced (CLK 500 and others)
Bi-xenon headlamps with cornering function introduced
mainEngines: OM642 DE30 LA - 3.0 V6 diesel (CLK 320 CDI, 224hp)
OM646 DE22 LA - 2.1 four-cylinder diesel (CLK 220 CDI, 150hp)
knownFor: M272 V6 balance shaft sprocket failure (P0016/P0017) - the single biggest CLK problem
OM642 V6 diesel - effortless torque but oil cooler seals fail
CLK 320 CDI is the most sought-after diesel variant
Diesels still coupé-only (no cabriolet diesel ever offered)
years.cta: Compare M272 and OM642 engine prices for 2005-2006 CLK
years.ticker: M272 balance shaft failure CLK 350 • CLK 320 CDI oil cooler seal • M272 engine replacement cost • CLK 280 engine price

years.preview: C209 Late Era: CLK 63 AMG & Final Evolution
years.description: Summer 2006 brought the ultimate CLK - the 481bhp CLK 63 AMG with Mercedes-AMG’s first in-house V8 - alongside engine upgrades across the range .
keyChanges: CLK 63 AMG (M156 6.2 V8, 481hp) replaced CLK 55 AMG
CLK 200 Kompressor upgraded from 163hp to 184bhp (M271 EVO)
CLK 500 received revised 5.5 V8 (M273, 388hp) in some markets
CLK 63 AMG Black Series (500hp, limited edition) released
Final CLK models produced before 2010 discontinuation
mainEngines: M271 E18 ML EVO - 1.8 supercharged petrol (CLK 200 Kompressor, 184hp)
OM642 DE30 LA - 3.0 V6 diesel (CLK 320 CDI, 224hp)
M156.984 - 6.2 V8 petrol (CLK 63 AMG, 481hp / Black Series 500hp)
knownFor: CLK 63 AMG M156 - head bolt failure risk on early engines (coolant ingress)
2006-onwards M272 engines have revised balance shaft sprockets (check engine number)
Black Series is ultra-rare and highly collectable
CLK production ended in 2010 with no direct successor
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2009 Mercedes CLK replacement engines
years.ticker: CLK 63 AMG engine replacement • M156 head bolt failure • CLK 200 Kompressor 184bhp engine price • OM642 V6 diesel rebuilt

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz CLK Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What is the Mercedes CLK350 balance shaft problem and which engines are affected?
items.answer: The M272 V6 balance shaft sprocket wears prematurely due to soft metal, causing timing chain slack, fault codes P0016/P0017, and eventual engine failure.
keyPoints: Affects CLK280 and CLK350 (2005-2007, M272 engines below serial #468933)
Symptoms: check engine light, rattling from front of engine on startup
Repair requires specialist disassembly - not a DIY job
2007+ facelift engines have hardened sprockets and are safe
items.cta: Compare rebuilt M272 engine prices for your Mercedes CLK

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mercedes CLK engine in the UK?
items.answer: Expect £950-£2,500 for a used unit, £1,200-£3,100 for rebuilt, plus £400-£800 for professional fitting. Final cost depends on engine code and damage severity.
keyPoints: Used CLK engines: £950-£2,500 supply only (mileage varies)
Rebuilt/reconditioned units: £1,200-£3,100 with 12-month warranty
M272 V6 and OM642 diesel are the most expensive to source
Supply & fit packages typically add £400-£800 to engine price
items.cta: Get instant Mercedes CLK engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Mercedes CLK320 CDI OM642 engine reliable? What common problems should I know about?
items.answer: The OM642 V6 diesel is smooth and torquey but has two well-known weaknesses - oil cooler seal failure and swirl flap linkage breakage - that require significant labour to fix.
keyPoints: Oil cooler seals leak at 80k-120k miles - requires intake manifold removal
Swirl flap linkage failure causes loss of power and stumbling below 1800rpm
No fault codes often stored - diagnosis requires live data monitoring
Once repaired with upgraded seals, the engine is otherwise robust
items.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 Mercedes CLK320 CDI engine prices

items.question: Does the Mercedes CLK200 Kompressor have timing chain problems?
items.answer: Yes, the M271 1.8-litre Kompressor engine is known for timing chain stretch and camshaft adjuster failure, often causing startup rattle and fault code P0020.
keyPoints: Timing chain rattle on cold start is the classic warning sign
Camshaft adjuster magnets fail, leading to poor running and limp mode
Chain and sprocket replacement costs £1,000-£1,800 at a specialist
Catch it early - a jumped chain bends valves and destroys the engine
items.cta: Find a reconditioned M271 engine for your Mercedes CLK200 Kompressor

items.question: What is the most reliable engine in the Mercedes CLK range?
items.answer: The M112 3.2 V6 (pre-2005 CLK320) and M113 V8s (CLK430, CLK500, CLK55 AMG) are widely considered Mercedes' most robust modern engines - the M272 is the one to avoid.
keyPoints: M112 3.2 V6 - bulletproof, no balance shaft issues, just crankshaft sensors fail
M113 V8 - robust, valve seal wear is the only common age-related issue
Avoid early M272 V6 (2005-2007) unless balance shaft recall confirmed
The 5-cylinder OM612 diesel (CLK270 CDI) is a cult favourite for reliability
items.cta: Compare prices for reliable Mercedes CLK engines by entering your registration

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my old Mercedes CLK?
items.answer: On a CLK worth £2,000-£5,000, specialist engine replacement at £1,500-£2,500 is worthwhile if the body and interior are in excellent condition - but only with a rebuilt unit that fixes the original design flaw.
keyPoints: CLKs are at "peak undesirability and value" - rock bottom prices
A clean, low-mileage CLK in good condition is worth saving
Rebuilt M272 with hardened sprockets costs £1,500-£3,100 supply only
If the car has rust, electrical issues, or poor bodywork, scrap it
items.cta: Get a value assessment and compare CLK engine replacement quotes

items.question: What causes loss of power on my Mercedes CLK350 with no warning lights?
items.answer: Common culprits include snapped intake manifold linkage rods (P0521 code), failed camshaft position sensors, or LPG installation issues - not always the balance shaft.
keyPoints: Plastic intake manifold linkage rods snap - £45 repair kit fixes it
Generic Chinese ignition coils often fail - fit genuine or quality brands
Cam sensors fail without storing permanent codes
Check VIN online to confirm if your engine is in the affected balance shaft range
items.cta: Diagnose your CLK's power loss - speak to a Mercedes engine specialist

items.question: How many miles will a Mercedes CLK engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained M112 V6 and M113 V8 regularly exceed 150k-200k miles. M272 V6 with balance shaft repaired will also last, but neglected M271 Kompressor units often fail before 100k.
keyPoints: M112/M113 - 150k-200k+ miles with regular servicing
M272 - indefinite if balance shaft replaced with hardened sprocket
OM642 diesel - 150k+ miles but oil cooler seals need doing once
M271 Kompressor - timing chain failure risk from 60k-100k miles
items.cta: Compare long-life rebuilt Mercedes CLK engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: What is the Mercedes CLK63 AMG head bolt problem?
items.answer: Early M156 6.2 V8 engines (pre-2010) have defective head bolts that can snap, allowing coolant into cylinders - catastrophic failure requiring full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Affects CLK63 AMG (2006-2009) - all pre-facelift M156 engines
Symptoms: unexplained coolant loss, misfire on cold start, white smoke
Head bolts can be replaced preventatively without engine removal
Rebuilt M156 engines come with updated Weistec or OEM head bolts
items.cta: Get quotes for M156 Mercedes CLK63 AMG replacement engines

items.question: What is the best Mercedes CLK engine to buy used?
items.answer: The 3.0 OM642 diesel (CLK320 CDI) offers the best balance of performance and economy, but check oil cooler seals. For petrol, the later 2007+ CLK350 with M272 balance shaft fix is safest.
keyPoints: CLK320 CDI (OM642) - 221hp, 45-50mpg, silky V6 torque
2007+ facelift CLK350 - balance shaft issue resolved, 272hp
CLK55 AMG (M113 V8) - bulletproof, values rising, future classic
Avoid 2005-2007 M272 engines without verified recall history
items.cta: Find a used Mercedes CLK engine with warranty - search our network
